title: Spectral
description: Adds Spectral dust and blocks that can be used to hide things in the Spectral, and goggles to let you see them!
body:
# Spectral

![A player switches between spectral states as they look at a sunset, seeing a sky full of phantoms while non-spectral, and spectral blocks spelling out "spectral" while spectral.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0552f72b5d65facb2cbd644710c38e7e74174f18.gif)

### Spectral is a mod that currently adds 4 items, three of which are pictured below:
![A player stands on a stone platform, looking at a stone wall with 3 item frames. The three item frames contain spectral dust, a pair of spectral goggles, and a spectral block](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6d448258c1c2bee1654dd2097e731d2fcc730a66.png)

The first, **Spectral Dust**, is crafted from phantom membranes and can either be eaten to turn temporarily spectral, or put onto a mob to turn it spectral.

The second, **Spectral Goggles**, put who wears them to enter a spectral state, allowing them to see and interact with spectral blocks and entities. These goggles can be crafted with Spectral Blocks, Spectral Dust, String, and Iron ingots.

The third, **Spectral Block** can be placed down as a normal block, but can only be seen, collided with, and broken by spectral players and mobs. (This function is per-player, so you might be able to see spectral blocks while your friends cannot!) This block can be crafted from Spectral Dust and glass!

The fourth and newest, **Block of Cold Iron** is the same as a spectral block but can only be interacted with by non-spectral entities!

## Mechanics

The basic mechanics of Spectral function as such:
There are two states living entities can be in, either normal (non-spectral) or spectral. This depends on their spectral_vision attribute (0 for normal, 1 for spectral). A non-spectral entity cannot see, hit, or push spectral entities, and it cannot break, collide, or see spectral blocks. Spectral entities cannot see, hit, or push non-spectral entities, but can fully interact with other spectral entities and spectral blocks.

## Known Issues:
-Spectral blocks do not properly change render states when sodium or other optimization mods that affect chunk rebuilds are installed
-Shadows appear ontop of Spectral blocks even when the player is not spectral
-If you encounter any other issues or have any suggestions, please post them on github issues for me to see!
